---
title: "Stacks"
date: "2025-11-16"
categories: ["Computer Science"]
--- 

## Introduction
A **stack** is a data type that supports the **push** (inserting a new element at the top of the stack) and **pop** (removing and returning the most recently added element at the top of the stack) operations. 

The behaviour of stacks is often called **last in, first out (LIFO)**.

Stacks are frequently used to implement depth-first search (DFS). 

In Python, stacks can be simulated using a list. 

### Corner Cases
* Empty stack (in particular, popping from an empty stack)
* Stack with one or two items

![](stacks.avif)

## Time Complexity

| Operation | Complexity | Notes |
| :--- | :--: | :--- |
| push | $O(1)$ | adding a new element to the end of the list (does not require shifting) |
| pop | $O(1)$ | removing the last element from the list (does not require shifting) |
